Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Alex, have you found a root cause for corruption?

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

>IO errors can be transient. this is not very common but happens, especially on complex storage with IB/FC interconnects.
unlink llogs are local files for MDT, and use journaling write under transaction, so it is not clear how header could be corrupted. Could you please explain?

Patch Set 2:

> unlink llogs are local files for MDT, and use journaling write under transaction, so it is not clear how header could be corrupted. Could you please explain?

storage controller failure, something bad may happen to surface, etc - lot of reasons.
Lustre is not the only source for corruptions.
I returned two WD SSDs (not even rotational ones) randomly corrupting data.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Thanks.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.

parents.subject: LU-4801 ldlm: discard l_lock from struct ldlm_lock.

commit.subject: LU-13450 llog: ignore EIO if request
commit.message:
LU-13450 llog: ignore EIO if request

unlink llogs aren't vital for normal processing
(at least until a filesystem has space available),
it doesn't make sense to fail to mount if unlink
llog is corrupted, better to warn about this, skip
correpted part and keep going.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I28a1a1420ab7c59904725670f46443ea08114e13
